findings.title: Repeated corrections in harness-dreams
findings.body: You corrected the agent 122 times this window around review. Encoding the rule once stops the loop.
findings.improvement: Turning a repeated correction into a written rule means you stop re-typing it every session.
findings.agentBenefit: The agent loads durable, project-local guidance before it picks tools or a plan.
findings.userBenefit: You spend fewer turns correcting assumptions, repeated questions, or missed state.
findings.reflection: Whether harness-dreams's corrections drops in the next cycle.
findings.evidence: “I dont likthe new one maybe be a littl eb more clean with the line on them and make them not lo…”
findings.action: Add a rule to harness-dreams AGENTS.md: When working on review, confirm the intended approach before large edits — this direction was corrected repeatedly.

findings.title: Tool errors slowed harness-dreams
findings.body: 36 tool calls surfaced errors this window. Capturing the working command keeps the agent from rediscovering it.
findings.improvement: Recording the command that actually works converts trial-and-error into a single reliable step.
findings.agentBenefit: The agent has the context it needs and stops guessing or repeating failures.
findings.userBenefit: You spend fewer turns correcting assumptions, repeated questions, or missed state.

findings.title: Re-ask rate is high in human-sense
findings.body: 67% of your turns were rapid corrections. Front-loading the goal and constraints cuts the back-and-forth.
findings.improvement: Stating the goal and constraints in the first prompt removes the rounds spent narrowing intent.
findings.agentBenefit: The agent has the context it needs and stops guessing or repeating failures.
findings.userBenefit: You spend fewer turns correcting assumptions, repeated questions, or missed state.
findings.reflection: Whether human-sense's friction drops in the next cycle.
findings.action: Start human-sense prompts with the goal + constraints up front to cut the 67% re-ask rate.
